author: DAN KOE
text:
A pattern I've noticed in stuck people:

They're always busy. They never stop moving. They have 47 tabs open and a notebook-sized to-do list. But if you ask them what they accomplished this week that actually matters, their mind goes blank.

Busyness isn't a badge of honor.

comments.author: DAN KOE
comments.text:
A pattern I've noticed in stuck people:

They're always busy. They never stop moving. They have 47 tabs open and a notebook-sized to-do list. But if you ask them what they accomplished this week that actually matters, their mind goes blank.

Busyness isn't a badge of honor.

comments.author: Atem
comments.text:
Another pattern I've noticed in stuck people:

Waiting for perfect conditions. They fail to understand that life is an infinite problem game. Your problems won't disappear so that you can focus on your goals.

You must act despite whatever you're going through.

comments.author: 𝗠𝗶𝗰𝗵𝗮𝗲𝗹 𝗞𝗼𝘃𝗲
comments.text:
It's an art of distraction.

Everything is a "research".

Best angle. Best copy. Best funnel. Best <insert shiny object>.

I know because I've been there.

And it's all "important" and "urgent"...

At one point it becomes the norm so when you sit down to do something that

comments.author: Grok
comments.text: Ready to bring ideas to life? Try Grok Imagine today

comments.author: Auny
comments.text: Hits home. I very recently started being aware of this. Being busy doesn’t mean you’re being productive. I started writing down tasks I wanna accomplish per day. And make sure they get done by the end of the day. Helps a lot.

comments.author: Chris Cunningham
comments.text: agree. fewer priorities, better results. always.

comments.author: Ken Guient
comments.text: Never been a big fan of people who brag about how busy they are. Would rather brag about freetime (if I were a bragger)

comments.author: Hispanic Nomad | Remote Work, Travel, Growth
comments.text:
47 tabs open is just a vision board for tasks you'll never do

We've all been there let's be honest

comments.author: Dev Shah
comments.text:
Ya, it is kinda show off

Like I am busy, but not that much to do something productive

comments.author: Logan Weaver
comments.text: Activity is often just a sophisticated way of hiding from high-leverage work.

comments.author: Saurabh
comments.text:
Super relatable. Too many people are going nowhere super fast.

Every successful person I know has a calm/relaxed aura.

comments.author: Adham Alkhaja
comments.text: 47 is a number AI loves, repels me from reading your content, my AI will though.

comments.author: Sharif Kayuyu
comments.text:
Action 

Set your self in motion, there is a way mobility makes you start connecting dots and making meaning to everything. 

Knowledge in its self is just.

 Application, tinkering, making mistakes then correcting them as you go is how we grow wisdom.
